steps.title: Identify the sentence type
steps.content: "What are atmospheric rivers, and how ___" is a direct question with two coordinated interrogative clauses joined by "and." A direct question must end with a question mark.

steps.title: Apply question word order
steps.content: A direct wh-question requires subject-auxiliary inversion: the auxiliary "do" comes before the subject "they" ("how do they affect"), not after it ("how they do affect," which is statement order).

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — do they affect our weather.: violates end-punctuation rule — a direct question needs a question mark, not a period. ✗
  • Choice B — they do affect our weather.: violates both rules — statement word order in a question, plus a period instead of a question mark. ✗
  • Choice C — do they affect our weather?: correct subject-auxiliary inversion and a question mark to close the direct question. ✓
  • Choice D — they do affect our weather?: violates word-order rule — statement order (subject before auxiliary) does not form a direct question. ✗
C is correct.
